input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 in some previews), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ and MAUI moved toward edge-to-edge rendering.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer about the recommended approach for .NET 10.


The deprecation of `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` stems from Android API 30 changes, and MAUI has shifted toward edge-to-edge layouts in .NET 10, which fundamentally changes how soft input interacts with the view hierarchy. The recommended solution now is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or properly handle insets to adjust the layout when the keyboard appears.

For a chat interface specifically, I'd wrap the content in a `Grid` with the input bar anchored at the bottom, then leverage `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` to automatically scroll the message list when the keyboard shows up—this works consistently across both Android and iOS in the current MAUI framework. The real problem in .NET 10 is that edge-to-edge is now the default on Android, which fundamentally changes how keyboard interactions work—the `Resize` mode that used to handle this has been deprecated at the platform level. I need to either rely on MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` (which should handle it automatically) or manually manage the insets when edge-to-edge is enabled.

For a chat screen specifically, I'd structure it with a CollectionView for messages on top and an input area at the bottom, then ensure `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` is connected to manage the keyboard behavior automatically.

With .NET 10 and edge-to-edge layouts, I need to configure the Android window soft input mode in `MauiProgram.cs` and handle insets properly with padding and margins.
